creator: vidwer@gmail.com
text:
2014-03-11 17:05 GMT+01:00 pizzamig <luca.pizzamiglio@gmail.com>:

> -----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
> Hash: SHA256
>
> I'm not able to reproduce the error.
>
> I've a FreeBSD 10.0 system and I build gdb without problems.
> It seems that the /usr/bin/gcc you're using has an erratic/fork
> behavior during the ADA support check., that causes the maxproc limit
>
> The main question is: what is the /usr/bin/gcc you are using? FreeBSD
> 10.0 is gcc free, it should use clang, not gcc....
> More specific, /usr/bin/gcc shouldn't exists...
>

Turns out that I symlinked/copied /usr/bin/clang to /usr/bin/gcc, removing
the duplicate solved the fork behaviour.
I don't have a machine with <10.0 (read: without clang as its base
compiler) to test with.
